NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO ENFORCE ORAL STIPULATION OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, TO SET ASIDE THE SPOUSAL PROPERTY ORDER, CALENDAR OF FEB-20-2020 - NO HEARING IS HELD, THE PARTIES APPEARED TODAY FOR THE SOLE PURPOSE OF OBTAINING THE PROPOSED ORDER. MR. WROBEL "APPROVES AS TO FORM" THE PROPOSED ORDER OUTSIDE THE PRESENCE OF THE COURT. THE PROPOSED ORDER IS REVIEWED AND SIGNED IN CHAMBERS BY THE COURT. THE TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED AS FOLLOWS: THE MOTION TO ENFORCE ORAL STIPULATION OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE VACATE ORDER IS GRANTED IN THE ALTERNATIVE. THEREFORE, THE AUGUST 21, 2019 ORDER GRANTING THE SPOUSAL PROPERTY PETITION IS VACATED. PETITIONER IS ORDERED PROMPTLY TO REVERSE AND UNDO ANY ACTIONS TAKEN ON THE BASIS OF THE COURT'S AUGUST 21, 2019 ORDER, INCLUDING THE RECORDING OF THAT ORDER. HEARING ON THE SPOUSAL PROPERTY PETITION AND OPPOSITION/OBJECTION TO IT IS RESET FOR APRIL 7, 2020. (SEE ORDER SIGNED THIS DATE FOR THE COURT'S COMPLETE RULING) - JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH. CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA. DEPUTY: E. LOPES.

calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petiton For Spousal Property
Off Calendar Jul-22-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thomas Wrobel appeared by telephone for Lordmark Zeino (Petitioner).
Tran La appeared by Zoom for Felix Shapiro (Objector).

Mr. Wrobel stated that his Client would like a decision, that his Client attempted to resolve the matter in light of the bad faith and ill will between the parties, that his Client does not want to wait any further and that they made an offer, but received no response. Ms. La expressed concern about the HELOC loan, stated that her Client is asking that the property not be transferred, that Mr. Zeino is not willing to take any obligation and that the Court has equitable powers to order that a creditor be paid out of the assets of the estate. The Court asked Ms. La how it can order what she is seeking when the property is community property, stated that the Court does not have discretion if it determines that the real property is community property and passes to the Spouse, that a remedy for anyone is to seek relief outside of the probate context in a civil matter and asked Ms. La if she has any authority to support her position. Ms. La stated that she does not have any authority that says the Court should not transfer property in order to protect a creditor's interest. Mr. Worth stated that there is no default in the payments what so ever, referred to an unrecorded deed and stated that he has records of payments from Mark. The Court granted the Amended Petition for Spousal Property (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Cabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petiton For Spousal Property

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petiton For Spousal Property
Off Calendar Mar-25-2020 Continued To Jun-30-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Oral Stipulation Or In The Alternative, To Set Aside The Spousal Property Order
Off Calendar Feb-20-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Trang La for Felix Shapiro.
Thomas Wrobel for Lordmark Zeino.

Objector Felix Shapiro's Notice of Motion and Motion to Enforce Oral Stipulation or in the Alternative, to Set Aside the Spousal Property Order.

No hearing is held, the parties appeared today for the sole purpose of obtaining the proposed order. Mr. Wrobel "approves as to form" the proposed order outside the presence of the Court. The proposed order is reviewed and signed in chambers by the Court.

The tentative ruling is adopted as follows: The Motion to Enforce Oral Stipulation or in the Alternative Vacate Order is granted in the alternative. The motion does not present, and the Court is not aware of, authority that would allow the Court to enforce by motion an oral settlement agreement resolving objections to a spousal property petition that the Court has already granted. The settlement agreement, even assuming that it could be enforced as a binding oral contract through a separate proceeding, does not satisfy the requirements of CCP section 664.6 for expedited enforcement by motion in the underlying action for a variety of reasons including that it is not written, not signed by the parties, and not incorporated into a court order retaining enforcement jurisdiction. Objector's motion requested that if the Court did not grant its request to enforce the settlement, it should vacate the order that was granted on the basis of the settlement. Petitioner does not object to that relief. Therefore, the August 21, 2019 Order granting the spousal property petition is vacated. Petitioner is ordered promptly to reverse and undo any actions taken on the basis of the Court's August 21, 2019 Order, including the recording of that order. Hearing on the spousal property petition and opposition/objection to it is reset for April 7, 2020.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: E. Lopes. Matter not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Monetary Sanctions Against Thomas Wrobel, Esq. For $6650.00
Off Calendar Feb-20-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Trang La for Felix Shapiro.
Thomas Wrobel for Lordmark Zeino.

Objector Felix Shapiro's Notice of Motion and Motion for Monetary Santions Against Thomas Wrobel, Esq. for $6650.00.

No hearing is held, the parties appeared today for the sole purpose of obtaining the proposed order. Mr. Wrobel "approves as to form" the proposed order outside the presence of the Court. The proposed order is reviewed and signed in chambers by the Court.

The tentative ruling is adopted as follows: The motion for sanctions is denied. Attorney Wrobel recited the terms of a settlement agreement and represented in open court that his client agreed to sell or refinance the subject property within eight months, in exchange for which promise Objector agreed to withdraw immediately objections to the spousal property petition, and, as a result, Petitioner obtained his spousal property order that day instead of at some later time. Petitioner himself was in court during these proceedings. Attorney Wrobel then denied having made the agreement on behalf of his client and denied that his client obtained relief on the basis of an agreement. Counsel either failed to obtain his client's consent to the agreement or misrepresented that consent to the Court and opposing party. The Court finds bad faith in either or both of the recital of an agreement without intending to abide by it in order to obtain desired relief and/or the denial of that agreement as soon as the relief was obtained. Nevertheless, sanctions are not appropriate on this record because Petitioner offered to vacate the order, the very relief Objector will obtain through this motion, prior to the motion being filed.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: E. Lopes. Matter not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Monetary Sanctions Against Thomas Wrobel, Esq. For $6650.00

Ex Parte Application To Enforce Oral Stipulation Or In The Alternative To Set Aside The Spousal Property Order
Off Calendar Oct-01-2019 Denied

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Aug-21-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thomas Wrobel for and with Lordmark Zeino (Petitioner).
Christine Tour-Sarkissian and Abigail Morris for Feliz Shapiro.

Mr. Wrobel stated that they had some discussions this morning, that the Objectors have agreed to withdraw their objections, and that although he feels they have no legal basis for their objections, they will stipulate to sell the property within eight months or refinance. Ms. Sarkissian stated that is it correct, that they will withdraw their objections, but it is predicated on the agreement and that she would like time to memorialize the agreement. Mr. Wrobel stated that he would like an order today removing the objections. The Court noted that only the spousal property petition is before it, that there is no petition for approval of a settlement agreement and that if the Parties are seeking approval, they will have to file a petition. The Court provided three options for the parties to consider. Ms. Sarkissian stated that she will trust Counsel and withdraws her objections. The objections are withdrawn and therefore, the Court granted the Petition for Spousal Property (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Mar-13-2019 Continued To May-08-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thomas Wrobel for and with Lordmark Zeino (Petitioner).
Trang La for Felix Shapiro (Objector).

The Court noted that an estate was opened in San Mateo and asked what the status is. Ms. La stated that she believes this Court does not have jurisdiction and that a hearing is set for April 24th in the San Mateo matter. Mr. Wrobel stated that there is no order in the San Mateo case, that the code requires exclusive jurisdiction for spousal property petitions which is the preferred method and that it is in San Francisco. The Court noted that there is no motion to dismiss this petition. The Amended Petition for Spousal Property is continued to May 8, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.
